analyst: manual enrichment from source code reading

complexity.rationale: Multiple parallel auth flows (password, Azure AD/EntraId via MSAL, SAML2, 2FA via SMS/email/TOTP), stateful UI slide-machine in login component, token refresh concurrency concern, impersonation nested context, per-customer SAML/SCIM configuration
high_complexity_areas: 2FA flow state machine — 5 distinct slide states with branching HTTP status codes (300, 428+authenticatorApp)
Token refresh — access token 15min, refresh token 30min, concurrency must be handled (parallel requests near expiry)
SAML2 per-customer SSO — EntityId + MetadataUrl + EmailClaim configuration per tenant
Impersonation — nested JWT identity with 'impersonateFromUserId' claim, original identity recovery

business_value.rationale: Foundation domain. Every other domain in the system depends on authenticated user identity for authorization, personalization, and audit trails. Zero-downtime migration is mandatory.
critical_dependencies: messaging — cannot send without authenticated user + profile
recipient_management — all recipient writes require userId on audit
subscriptions — subscription ownership linked to user
reporting — all reports scoped to customerId from user context

outbound_integrations: Email service (password reset, 2FA pin, SAML user provisioning emails)
coupling.note: Highest inbound coupling of all 49 domains. Every authenticated request passes through this domain's token validation.

rebuild_risk.rationale: Any regression in authentication or authorization instantly breaks all user-facing functionality across all domains. The 2FA flow state machine is intricate and easy to break silently.

top_risks.risk: JWT claim structure change breaks all downstream authorization
top_risks.mitigation: Do NOT change claim names or structure during Blazor migration. Keep existing token shape.

top_risks.risk: Token refresh race condition if multiple concurrent API calls near expiry
top_risks.mitigation: Implement SemaphoreSlim(1,1) lock around refresh call in IHttpClientFactory DelegatingHandler

top_risks.risk: SAML2 customer SSO config regression silently locks out entire customer
top_risks.mitigation: Add per-tenant integration test with mock IdP before migration go-live

top_risks.risk: SCIM provisioning break disables IdP-synced user accounts
top_risks.mitigation: Smoke test ScimUsersController + ScimGroupsController after each deployment

rebuild_strategy: Strangler Fig — replace one auth flow at a time while keeping the existing ASP.NET Core backend API intact. Migration sequence: (1) email/password login + token storage in Blazor, (2) token refresh interceptor, (3) 2FA flows, (4) Azure AD / MSAL, (5) SAML2, (6) SCIM. Do NOT migrate backend until all Blazor frontend auth is verified end-to-end.

technical_debt_to_address.description: Access token TTL (15 min) and refresh token TTL (30 min) are hardcoded TimeSpan constants in UserController — not environment-configurable
technical_debt_to_address.fix: Extract to IOptions<TokenSettings> and configure via appsettings.json

technical_debt_to_address.description: Logout does NOT invalidate the JWT access token server-side — only deletes UserRefreshToken. A logged-out user retains a valid JWT for up to 15 minutes.
technical_debt_to_address.fix: Implement short-lived token denylist (Redis or DB table with TTL) checked in JWT middleware, or reduce access token TTL to 2-5 minutes

technical_debt_to_address.description: UpdateUser endpoint always returns BadRequest — missing return Ok() after successful update
technical_debt_to_address.fix: Add return Ok() at end of success branch in UserController.UpdateUser()

technical_debt_to_address.description: isRunningInIE() check still present in BiLoginComponent — IE is end-of-life since June 2022
technical_debt_to_address.fix: Remove all IE detection code during Blazor rebuild

technical_debt_to_address.description: AuthenticatorSecret stored as plain string on User entity — no indication of encryption at rest
technical_debt_to_address.fix: Encrypt with IDataProtectionProvider before persisting; decrypt on read

technical_debt_to_address.description: No MFA recovery codes documented — user permanently locked out if 2FA device is lost
technical_debt_to_address.fix: Implement one-time recovery codes (hashed, stored in separate table) as fallback

unit_tests: UserController.Login() — happy path, wrong password, lockout threshold, multi-profile (300), 2FA required (428)
UserController.RefreshAccessToken() — valid token, expired token, not-found token
TokenRefresh concurrency — two simultaneous calls near expiry must not issue two refresh tokens
integration_tests: Full email/password login → access protected endpoint → refresh → logout
Impersonation → protected endpoint → cancel → original identity restored
SCIM: create user via POST /scim/users → verify User created in DB
e2e_tests: Blazor LoginPage — submit valid credentials → redirect to home
Blazor LoginPage — wrong password 5x → locked message
Blazor 2FA flow — slide 0 → 428 → slide 1 → submit code → logged in
